Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Request For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jul-26-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES: EUNJI C. CHO, ESQ. of Low, Ball & Lynch (Tel: 415-981-6630) appearing for defendant California Fleet Maintenance, Inc.;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING: PLAINTIFF OFA TAIMANI'S Motion To Quash Subpoenas And Request For Protective Order.

Matter submitted without argument. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: It was not a "reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve informally any dispute concerning discovery," CCP 2023.010(f), for Plaintiff to send a meet-and-confer letter, receive a substantive response, and then "decline[] to respond." MPA at 4. Similarly, as Defendant points out, CRC 3.1345 requires a separate statement to support a motion to quash a subpoena - particularly when the motion relates to the content of the subpoena. Failure to file a separate statement supports denying the motion. Mills v. U.S. Bank (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 871, 892. Either of these failures by Plaintiff would support denying the motion. However, considering the privacy issues implicated, and Defendant's willingness to consider more tailored subpoenas, the motion to quash is GRANTED. Defendant is permitted to serve revised subpoenas that comport with the offer set out in the Cho letter of April 22, 2019. The parties are free to agree to a stipulation for a protective order.

Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported (302/JPT)
